clinical_history: ECG from a 40 year old woman a severe underlying condition and multiple prior admissions. What is the rhythm and which ONE of the following diagnoses is most likely?
diagnosis: This ECG shows atrial flutter with predominant 2:1 conduction that is variable at times with an average ventricular rate of about 120/min. There is generalized low voltage with marked right axis deviation and a right ventricular conduction delay. Nonspecific ST-T changes are present along with slow initial R wave progression in the chest leads. This constellation of findings is most consistent with (but not diagnostic of) obesity/hypoventilation (Pickwickian) syndrome. Echocardiogram showed marked right ventricular enlargement and hypokinesis, as well as evidence of moderate pulmonary hypertension.
